evals.name: yoruba-mining-recipe
evals.prompt: I have ~3K real En-Yoruba parallel pairs. I want to build a bigger MT corpus. What's the recipe?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) Class 2 target → recommend SONAR over LASER3 (Niger-Congo coverage); (2) margin threshold 1.04 + spot-check 50 (NOT 1.06); (3) length-ratio [0.5, 2.0]; (4) synthetic bitext ESSENTIAL — recommend back-translation T=0.9, dictionary substitution if lexicon available, pivot via En-Sw-Yor possibly; (5) flag Bible-NLP register risk if used; (6) hand off to magic-linguistic-tokenize for fertility audit + magic-linguistic-transfer for LoRA planning.

evals.name: polysynthetic-length-ratio-trap
evals.prompt: I'm aligning English-Inuktitut parallel data and 60% of pairs are getting filtered as misaligned because the Inuktitut side is much shorter (length ratio ~0.3). Bug?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NOT a bug — Inuktitut is polysynthetic; ratio 0.3 is correct; (2) recommend widening length-ratio filter to [0.2, 1.5]; (3) explain polysynthetic morphology compresses meaning; (4) note typological-distance-aware filtering should already be applied via magic-linguistic-scope output; (5) recommend manual spot-check to confirm valid pairs are surviving.

evals.name: bible-only-register-drift
evals.prompt: Our En-Twi MT model trained on Bible-only parallel data sounds weirdly archaic in casual settings. Why?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) immediately diagnose register drift from Bible-only training; (2) explain Bible/liturgical register dominates and bleeds into general output; (3) recommend supplementing with at least 10% non-Bible parallel (news, conversations, web); (4) note this is the dominant low-resource MT failure mode; (5) suggest synthetic back-translation from Twi monolingual web/news to expand register; (6) note this should have been flagged at corpus + bitext audit time.
